description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

【REGISTRATION CLOSED】 Registration ended as of May 20, 2026.

The Hanno City Board of Education is hosting a Cultural Heritage Walk on Saturday, May 23, 2026 (Reiwa 8), from 9:15 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. The theme is "Enjoying Hanno's Landforms and Geology."

Meet at Moto-Kaji Station on the Seibu Ikebukuro Line. The group will walk approximately 3 kilometers to Akebono Children's Forest Park while observing fossil footprints of the Akebono elephant in the Iruma River riverbed. Expert commentary will explain the Hanno and Butsuji geological layers that form the Kaji Hills.

Eligibility: High school students and older
Capacity: 20 people (capacity already reached—no new applications accepted)
Fee: ¥500 (includes insurance)

What to bring: Comfortable clothes, hat, sturdy walking shoes, drinking water, and other heat illness prevention supplies.

Conditions: Proceeds in light rain; cancelled if weather is severe.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyoikubu/hakubutsukan/bunkazaitanto/bunkazai/13272.html
summary: ℹ️ Cultural Heritage Walk scheduled for May 23, 2026 (Reiwa 8) – Registration closed as of May 20
